data.title: HEROAD Unleashes a New Era of Gaming: Professional-Grade Gear Meets Unbeatable Value
data.description: In Shenzhen, China, on March 20, 2026, QCY introduced the HEROAD e-sports brand to revolutionize the competitive gaming industry. HEROAD, based on the mantra "For Gamers. For Winners," aims to transform the gaming landscape. Built to bridge existing gaps, the brand is set to make a significant impact.

data.title: Bitcoin lags behind as global money supply grows due to rising energy expenses and tighter financial conditions.
data.description: The increase in fuel prices and strict financial conditions are consuming consumer liquidity, which is one of the reasons why the growth in the global money supply has not led to an increase in Bitcoin value.

data.title: Bringing the World to Chiayi: 2026 CIADFF Sets the Stage with 55 Global Works
data.description: The 13th Chiayi International Art Documentary Film Festival (CIADFF) commenced on March 6th at the Chiayi City Museum of Art in Chiayi City. Mayor Huang Min-hui presided over the opening ceremony alongside ambassadors and diplomatic representatives from eight countries, such as Poland.

data.title: Lovense Integrates OpenClaw, Pioneering a New Era of AI Agents in Intimacy Technology
data.description: In Los Angeles on March 19, 2026, a growing trend towards autonomous AI agents over passive AI products like chatbots and virtual assistants is observed with the emergence of OpenClaw. More users are opting for personalized and needs-driven AI solutions. The shift indicates a rise in users preferring approximately 1000 characters.

data.title: GI Innovation Announces First Clinical Supply Agreement to Explore Combination Therapy for Prostate Cancer
data.description: On March 19, 2026, in Seoul, South Korea, GI Innovation reported entering a clinical supply agreement with Johnson & Johnson to assess a combination therapy for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) in a phase 1b clinical trial. Key details include the collaboration being formed on the 19th, the involvement of GI Innovation and Johnson & Johnson, and the focus on evaluating the therapy in mCRPC patients.
